title: Payroll Modernization Overview -- AS400 Manual Processes, Employee Self-Service Vision, and HCM System Architecture
summary: Finance Committee convenes long-awaited payroll modernization presentation. Josh Syrson (API project manager), CFO, and Mike Smith (Assistant Director HR) present objectives: (1) improved business processes -- current employee status changes require printing AS400 screenshot, handwriting changes, scanning, DocuSigning (10-15 min per change vs seconds in modern system); employee self-service for address updates, open enrollment, direct deposit; (2) sustainable payroll management -- weekly + bi-weekly + SURA payroll cycles, paper time sheets from 2,000-person organization, 2 retirees + 3 staff in payroll, recently processed 24 police officer retropay corrections dating to 2021 (40 staff hours for 24 corrections); (3) workforce analytics -- overtime cost analysis currently impossible, compliance tracking across departments, data-driven scheduling. System diagram: timekeeping (SWC/TeleStaff) and HCM (HR + payroll) shown as cyclical process; AS400 handles demographic data and payroll but lacks position management, applicant tracking, employee self-service. Current demographic data described as 'incomplete at best' and 'isolated' -- cannot generate reports or track work authorization status. Nine union contracts with complex deduction cascades make every personnel change multi-step.

processing_notes: HR, Fire, Finance, IT, API in detected_departments are all contextually relevant; API, HR, Finance are presenting departments
TeleStaff = public safety scheduling software being used for payroll (not its intended purpose)
'Secondary jobs' = AS400 term for contractual add-ons (uniform allowance, sick time buyback, over-the-cap bonus)
SURA = Syracuse Urban Renewal Agency (separate payroll cycle)
CFO first name 'Mike' mentioned but last name not stated in transcript
Segment 1 of 7 from Finance Committee Meeting, Tuesday, February 3, 2026
